text: Fill in the blanks about the temporal and infratemporal regions.
questions: All four muscles of mastication are supplied by the *mandibular/CN V3:third division of the trigeminal nerve* division of the trigeminal nerve.
The only muscle of mastication that can depress (open) the mandible is the *lateral pterygoid:muscle with superior and inferior heads*.
The maxillary artery is the terminal branch of the *external carotid:parent artery* artery and is the dominant vessel of the infratemporal fossa.
The pterygoid venous plexus in the infratemporal fossa communicates with the *cavernous:intracranial venous sinus* sinus, providing a route for spread of dental infections intracranially.

In TMJ dislocation, the mandibular condyle slips anterior to the *articular eminence:bony ridge that normally prevents excessive anterior movement* and is locked by spasm of the lateral pterygoid muscle.
